# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: latex-lsp/texlab.wiki
项目名: texlab.wiki
讲义目录: texlab.wiki-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/
当前 HEAD: bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "texlab.wiki",
  "rationale": "本仓库是 texlab（LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器）的官方 wiki，共 7 个页面，本身就是文档而非源码，因此讲义的“源码”即这 7 篇 wiki，实践任务以“写配置/搭集成/调用命令/验证行为”为主。学习顺序按“先建立全局认知，再深入配置与集成，最后理解协议扩展与可扩展点”设计：单元 1 用 Home 与 Project-Detection 让读者先搞清 texlab 是什么、如何识别一个 LaTeX 工程（这是几乎所有功能的前置）；单元 2 引入配置模型（命名空间、类型、占位符）这一后续所有讲义的公共语言，并聚焦最常用的构建配置；单元 3 围绕“编译→预览”主链路展开，涵盖编译流程、SyncTeX 双向搜索与各阅读器集成、以及 tectonic 替代引擎；单元 4 进入 LSP 协议层，讲清 texlab 的自定义请求、workspace 命令与结构→符号种类映射；单元 5 面向高级配置与二次开发，把 Configuration.md 中较零散的诊断/符号/补全/格式化/inlay hints/experimental 扩展点按主题聚合。讲义依赖遵循“先懂配置模型才能读具体项”“先懂项目识别才能讲依赖图与标签前缀”“先懂编译流程才能讲自定义 build 请求”等关系，避免读者跳读时缺失前置概念。Configuration.md 内容庞大（约 40 项设置横跨构建/诊断/符号/格式化等多个子系统），故按子系统拆成多篇，而非一篇过载。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "初识 texlab：定位与项目模型",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "texlab 是什么：项目定位与 wiki 导览",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-texlab-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 texlab 是一个为编辑器提供 LaTeX/BibTeX 智能能力的语言服务器，采用 LSP 客户端-服务器架构；并导览本 wiki 的 7 个页面分别讲什么，帮助读者建立全局认知。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 texlab 的定位：基于 LSP 的 LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器",
            "掌握 LSP 客户端-服务器架构，以及 texlab、TeX 引擎、PDF 阅读器各自的角色",
            "能说出 Home/Configuration/LSP-Internals/Previewing/Project-Detection/Tectonic/Workspace-commands 各页面的主题与建议阅读顺序"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Home.md",
            "Configuration.md",
            "LSP-Internals.md",
            "Project-Detection.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "项目定位",
            "LSP 客户端-服务器架构",
            "Wiki 导览"
          ],
          "practice_task": "通读本 wiki 全部 7 个页面，画一张关系图描述「编辑器(LSP 客户端) ↔ texlab(LSP 服务器) ↔ TeX 引擎 ↔ PDF 阅读器」四者如何交互，并用一句话标注每个 wiki 页面对应 texlab 的哪一项能力。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "项目识别与根目录检测",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-project-detection.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 texlab 如何从单个 .tex 文件发现整个「项目」（编译进同一文档的所有文件）——通过向上遍历目录树、解析 \\input/\\import 构建依赖树；以及通过 .texlabroot / Tectonic.toml / .latexmkrc / 根源文件四步优先级确定根目录。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解「项目」概念与依赖树（\\input/\\import）的迭代构建过程",
            "掌握根目录检测的四步优先级算法及其各自的影响（输出目录、src/build 目录、latexmkrc 推断等）",
            "理解为何必须确定根目录：TeX 引擎的 \\input 等命令基于工作目录而非源文件目录"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Project-Detection.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Discovery 算法",
            "依赖树解析",
            "根目录检测四步法"
          ],
          "practice_task": "创建一个含 main.tex（带 \\documentclass 与 document 环境）并用 \\input 引入 chapter1.tex 的多文件工程；分别在父目录放置 .texlabroot、Tectonic.toml、.latexmkrc，预测并验证 texlab 会选中哪个目录作为根目录。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "配置基础：理解 texlab.* 配置模型",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "配置总览：命名空间、类型与占位符",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-config-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 texlab 的配置模型：配置由 LSP 客户端持有、服务器通过查询获取；配置项统一在 texlab.* 命名空间下，每项有 Type 与 Default value，部分项支持 %f/%p/%l 占位符。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解配置由 LSP 客户端持有、服务器查询的机制",
            "学会阅读每个配置项的 Type / Default value / Placeholders 三要素",
            "掌握 %f（TeX 文件路径）、%p（PDF 路径）、%l（行号）三个占位符的含义与出现位置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Configuration.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "配置查询机制",
            "texlab.* 命名空间",
            "占位符 %f/%p/%l"
          ],
          "practice_task": "为你的编辑器写一份最小 texlab 配置 JSON，至少包含 build.executable、build.args（带 %f）、forwardSearch.args（带 %f/%p/%l），并为每个字段注释其类型与默认值。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "构建配置 texlab.build.*",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-build-config.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "逐项讲解 texlab.build.* 配置：executable、args、onSave、forwardSearchAfter、useFileList、auxDirectory/logDirectory/pdfDirectory、filename，以及已弃用的 texlab.auxDirectory 与 latexmkrc 自动推断的关系。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握切换构建工具（latexmk / 其他）的方法与 args 中 flag 与参数需拆分为数组元素的约定",
            "理解 onSave、forwardSearchAfter、useFileList 各自的作用与性能权衡",
            "学会配置 auxDirectory/logDirectory/pdfDirectory 输出目录，并理解使用 latexmkrc 时 texlab 会自动推断"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Configuration.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "build.executable / build.args",
            "构建触发（onSave / forwardSearchAfter）",
            "输出目录配置与 latexmkrc 推断"
          ],
          "practice_task": "编写一份配置让 texlab 用 latexmk 把 PDF 输出到 ./build 目录、保存时自动编译；再说明若改用非 latexmk 工具，需要额外显式设置哪些目录项及为何。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "编译、预览与 SyncTeX 集成",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "编译与预览的整体流程",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-build-and-preview-workflow.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 texlab 编译文档的两种触发方式（textDocument/build 自定义请求与 onSave 自动构建）、forwardSearchAfter 如何衔接正向搜索，以及 SyncTeX 的角色；说明为何推荐 onSave 而非 latexmk 的 -pvc。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解编译的两种触发路径（自定义请求 vs onSave）及其区别",
            "掌握 forwardSearchAfter 串起的「编译→正向搜索」工作流",
            "理解 SyncTeX 在编辑器与 PDF 阅读器之间双向跳转中的作用，以及为何不用 latexmk -pvc"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Configuration.md",
            "Previewing.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "编译触发方式",
            "forwardSearchAfter 工作流",
            "SyncTeX 概念"
          ],
          "practice_task": "配置 texlab.build.onSave=true 与 forwardSearchAfter=true，在编辑器中修改并保存一个 LaTeX 文件，观察「保存→编译→跳转到 PDF」完整链路，并记录每一步由哪个配置项驱动。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2",
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "SyncTeX 双向搜索与 PDF 阅读器集成",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-synctex-viewers.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 texlab.forwardSearch.* 配置，并给出 SumatraPDF/Evince/Okular/Zathura/qpdfview/Skim/Sioyek 等阅读器的正向搜索与逆向搜索配置；介绍 texlab inverse-search 子命令及其与 window/showDocument 的关系。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 forwardSearch.executable / forwardSearch.args 与 %f/%p/%l 占位符的用法",
            "能为自己平台上的 PDF 阅读器配置正向搜索（编辑器→PDF）",
            "理解逆向搜索（PDF→编辑器）如何通过 texlab inverse-search 子命令回跳"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Previewing.md",
            "Configuration.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [

            "各阅读器正向搜索配方",

            "u3-l1"
          ],

          "id": "u3-l3",
          "title": "使用 Tectonic 作为替代 TeX 引擎",

          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解如何把构建工具切换为 tectonic（V2 与 V1 两种 CLI 写法），为何建议加 --keep-intermediates / --keep-logs，以及如何让 pdfDirectory/auxDirectory 与 --outdir 对齐；并联系 Project-Detection 中 Tectonic.toml 的根目录检测。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 tectonic V2（-X compile）与 V1 CLI 的 texlab.build.args 写法",
            "理解 --keep-intermediates（补全章节编号）与 --keep-logs（上报编译告警）各自的作用",
            "学会把 pdfDirectory/auxDirectory 与 tectonic --outdir 对齐，并理解 Tectonic.toml 对项目识别的影响"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Tectonic.md",
            "Configuration.md",
            "Project-Detection.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "tectonic V2 / V1 CLI",
            "keep-intermediates / keep-logs",
            "输出目录对齐与 Tectonic.toml"
          ],
          "practice_task": "把一份现有 latexmk 配置改写为 tectonic（任选 V1 或 V2），保证 pdfDirectory/auxDirectory 指向同一输出目录；编译后验证 texlab 仍能显示章节编号与编译告警。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u4",
      "title": "LSP 协议扩展：自定义消息与工作区命令",
      "level": "advanced",
      "order": 4,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u4-l1",
          "title": "自定义 LSP 消息：build 与 forwardSearch",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u4-l1-custom-lsp-messages.md",
          "level": "advanced",
          "topic": "讲解 texlab 对 LSP 的两处自定义扩展：textDocument/build（编译请求，返回 BuildStatus）与 textDocument/forwardSearch（正向搜索请求，返回 ForwardSearchStatus），以及用 experimental capability 声明这些可选请求的机制。",
          "learning_goals": [

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 texlab 是什么：项目定位与 wiki 导览

本讲是整本手册的起点，确立 texlab 定位并导览 wiki。核心结论：texlab 是基于 LSP（语言服务器协议）的 LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器，既非编辑器、也非 TeX 引擎或 PDF 阅读器，而是把三者缝合的协调者。LaTeX 工作流由四个角色协作：编辑器（LSP 客户端）、texlab（LSP 服务器）、TeX 引擎、PDF 阅读器。其中只有编辑器↔texlab 走标准 LSP（JSON-RPC）加 texlab 自定义扩展；texlab 与引擎、阅读器之间则靠调用外部命令（executable + args + %f/%p/%l 占位符）交互，由 texlab.build 与 texlab.forwardSearch 配置驱动。关键认知：配置由 LSP 客户端持有、texlab 按需查询。本仓库是纯文档 wiki，共 7 个页面，建议顺序为 Home→Project-Detection→Configuration→Previewing→Tectonic→LSP-Internals→Workspace-commands，其中 Configuration 最厚且横跨多子系统。引入的关键术语包括 LSP、语言服务器、协调者、SyncTeX、tectonic、自定义消息、枚举映射、workspace/executeCommand，供后续讲义承接。
### u1-l2 项目识别与根目录检测

本讲回答 texlab 如何识别「项目」并确定「根目录」，覆盖三个模块。

**项目**指编译进同一份文档的全部 `.tex` 文件。**Discovery 算法**从打开的文件出发，每轮向上遍历目录树加载文件、并解析 `\input`/`\import` 拉入被引用文件，单调增长直到 workspace 不变（求不动点，因磁盘文件有限必然收敛）。解析结果组织成以包含命令为边、以**根文档**（含 `document` 环境的最顶层主文件）为根的**依赖树**；因 `\input` 可任意嵌套，必须迭代展开，单趟扫描必漏文件。

**根目录**之所以必须确定，是因为 TeX 引擎的 `\input` 基于**工作目录**而非源文件目录，texlab 需替你把工作目录设对。四步优先级算法从当前目录向上探测、命中即停：`.texlabroot` → `Tectonic.toml` → `.latexmkrc` → 根源文件（排除 `\documentclass{subfiles}`），前三步还连带决定构建产物目录（`auxDirectory`/`logDirectory`/`pdfDirectory`，latexmkrc 可自动推断、失败兜底）的来源。根目录既是编译起点，也是 Discovery 向上扫描的「天花板」，二者耦合。
### u2-l1 配置总览：命名空间、类型与占位符

本讲是 texlab「配置」主题的地基，建立读懂任意配置项的通用语言，不逐项讲语义。三个最小模块：第一，**配置查询机制**——texlab 不读任何配置文件、也不规定格式；配置由 **LSP 客户端（编辑器）持有**，texlab 作为服务器在需要时反向查询获取（默认值兜底，故零配置即可编译）。第二，**`texlab.*` 命名空间**——所有键共享 `texlab.` 前缀，按 `texlab.<分类>.<键>` 分层组织；每项都可用「**三要素**」读懂：Type（类型）、Default value（默认值）、Placeholders（占位符，仅部分项有）。第三，**占位符 `%f`/`%p`/`%l`**——由 texlab 在执行外部命令前替换：`%f`（TeX 文件路径）出现在 `build.args` 与 `forwardSearch.args`；`%p`（PDF 路径）、`%l`（行号）仅出现在 `forwardSearch.args`。另需记住一条拆分规则：flag 与其参数必须拆成数组独立元素。后续讲义将沿用「配置归客户端」「三要素」「三占位符」这套语言逐项展开。
### u2-l2 构建配置 texlab.build.*

本讲是配置通用语言首次落到完整子系统 `texlab.build.*`（texlab 如何把 `.tex` 编译成 PDF），含三个最小模块：其一，`build.executable`/`build.args` 决定「谁来编译、怎么编译」，默认 `latexmk` 加 `["-pdf","-interaction=nonstopmode","-synctex=1","%f"]`，`%f` 是 `build.args` 唯一占位符，换工具必须换 args，且 flag 与参数须拆成数组独立元素。其二，布尔开关 `onSave`（保存即编译）、`forwardSearchAfter`（编译后顺手做正向搜索）默认均为 `false`，后者需配合 `forwardSearch.*`；因 texlab 不会被 latexmk 通知编译完成，官方不推荐用 `-pvc`。其三，产物目录 `auxDirectory`/`logDirectory`/`pdfDirectory` 默认 `.`（根目录）：用 `latexmkrc` 时 texlab 自动推断产物目录（latexmkrc 同时是根目录标志），非 latexmk 工具则必须「两头对齐」——在 `args` 让引擎输出到该目录、又在这些设置项告知 texlab 去找。另含 `filename`（覆盖 PDF 名）、`useFileList`（用 `.fls` 反哺项目检测，有性能代价）及已弃用的顶层 `texlab.auxDirectory`。

---

   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 texlab 是什么：项目定位与 wiki 导览

本讲是整本手册的起点，确立 texlab 定位并导览 wiki。核心结论：texlab 是基于 LSP（语言服务器协议）的 LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器，既非编辑器、也非 TeX 引擎或 PDF 阅读器，而是把三者缝合的协调者。LaTeX 工作流由四个角色协作：编辑器（LSP 客户端）、texlab（LSP 服务器）、TeX 引擎、PDF 阅读器。其中只有编辑器↔texlab 走标准 LSP（JSON-RPC）加 texlab 自定义扩展；texlab 与引擎、阅读器之间则靠调用外部命令（executable + args + %f/%p/%l 占位符）交互，由 texlab.build 与 texlab.forwardSearch 配置驱动。关键认知：配置由 LSP 客户端持有、texlab 按需查询。本仓库是纯文档 wiki，共 7 个页面，建议顺序为 Home→Project-Detection→Configuration→Previewing→Tectonic→LSP-Internals→Workspace-commands，其中 Configuration 最厚且横跨多子系统。引入的关键术语包括 LSP、语言服务器、协调者、SyncTeX、tectonic、自定义消息、枚举映射、workspace/executeCommand，供后续讲义承接。
### u1-l2 项目识别与根目录检测

本讲回答 texlab 如何识别「项目」并确定「根目录」，覆盖三个模块。

**项目**指编译进同一份文档的全部 `.tex` 文件。**Discovery 算法**从打开的文件出发，每轮向上遍历目录树加载文件、并解析 `\input`/`\import` 拉入被引用文件，单调增长直到 workspace 不变（求不动点，因磁盘文件有限必然收敛）。解析结果组织成以包含命令为边、以**根文档**（含 `document` 环境的最顶层主文件）为根的**依赖树**；因 `\input` 可任意嵌套，必须迭代展开，单趟扫描必漏文件。

**根目录**之所以必须确定，是因为 TeX 引擎的 `\input` 基于**工作目录**而非源文件目录，texlab 需替你把工作目录设对。四步优先级算法从当前目录向上探测、命中即停：`.texlabroot` → `Tectonic.toml` → `.latexmkrc` → 根源文件（排除 `\documentclass{subfiles}`），前三步还连带决定构建产物目录（`auxDirectory`/`logDirectory`/`pdfDirectory`，latexmkrc 可自动推断、失败兜底）的来源。根目录既是编译起点，也是 Discovery 向上扫描的「天花板」，二者耦合。
### u2-l1 配置总览：命名空间、类型与占位符

本讲是 texlab「配置」主题的地基，建立读懂任意配置项的通用语言，不逐项讲语义。三个最小模块：第一，**配置查询机制**——texlab 不读任何配置文件、也不规定格式；配置由 **LSP 客户端（编辑器）持有**，texlab 作为服务器在需要时反向查询获取（默认值兜底，故零配置即可编译）。第二，**`texlab.*` 命名空间**——所有键共享 `texlab.` 前缀，按 `texlab.<分类>.<键>` 分层组织；每项都可用「**三要素**」读懂：Type（类型）、Default value（默认值）、Placeholders（占位符，仅部分项有）。第三，**占位符 `%f`/`%p`/`%l`**——由 texlab 在执行外部命令前替换：`%f`（TeX 文件路径）出现在 `build.args` 与 `forwardSearch.args`；`%p`（PDF 路径）、`%l`（行号）仅出现在 `forwardSearch.args`。另需记住一条拆分规则：flag 与其参数必须拆成数组独立元素。后续讲义将沿用「配置归客户端」「三要素」「三占位符」这套语言逐项展开。
### u2-l2 构建配置 texlab.build.*

本讲是配置通用语言首次落到完整子系统 `texlab.build.*`（texlab 如何把 `.tex` 编译成 PDF），含三个最小模块：其一，`build.executable`/`build.args` 决定「谁来编译、怎么编译」，默认 `latexmk` 加 `["-pdf","-interaction=nonstopmode","-synctex=1","%f"]`，`%f` 是 `build.args` 唯一占位符，换工具必须换 args，且 flag 与参数须拆成数组独立元素。其二，布尔开关 `onSave`（保存即编译）、`forwardSearchAfter`（编译后顺手做正向搜索）默认均为 `false`，后者需配合 `forwardSearch.*`；因 texlab 不会被 latexmk 通知编译完成，官方不推荐用 `-pvc`。其三，产物目录 `auxDirectory`/`logDirectory`/`pdfDirectory` 默认 `.`（根目录）：用 `latexmkrc` 时 texlab 自动推断产物目录（latexmkrc 同时是根目录标志），非 latexmk 工具则必须「两头对齐」——在 `args` 让引擎输出到该目录、又在这些设置项告知 texlab 去找。另含 `filename`（覆盖 PDF 名）、`useFileList`（用 `.fls` 反哺项目检测，有性能代价）及已弃用的顶层 `texlab.auxDirectory`。

1	`texlab` supports compiling LaTeX using a custom request (`textDocument/build`)
2	and by building a document after saving if configured to do so.
3	To enable building on save, simply set `texlab.build.onSave` to true.
4	Previewing can be configured in a variety of ways:
5	
6	1. If you are using `latexmk`, you can create a `.latexmkrc` file and call your viewer accordingly.
7	   Afterwards, you can add the `-pv` flag to your `texlab.build.args`.
8	
9	2. If you want the PDF viewer to stay synchronized with the cursor position in your editor,
10	   you can instruct `texlab` to execute a forward search after every build (`texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter`).
11	   To do so, you need to enable [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf)
12	   and update the `texlab.forwardSearch` configuration.
13	   If you want to use this feature, we do _not_ recommend the `-pvc` flag
14	   because `texlab` does not get notified by `latexmk` when a document gets built.
15	   Instead, you can use `texlab.build.onSave`.
16	
17	In the following sections, we will give SyncTeX configurations for several popular viewers.
18	If your viewer is not listed here, you can send us a pull request or create an issue.
19	
20	
21	If you editor supports the `window/showDocument` request, 
22	then you can use `texlab inverse-search --input <FILE> --line <LINE>` to forward the inverse search to the editor.
23	
24	In this section, we will give instructions for the `texlab inverse-search` subcommand. 
25	If your editor does not support it or the path of `texlab` is not fixed/known, then you need to adapt the commands to your editor.
26	Some plugins can help with setting up inverse search:
27	
28	- `Neovim`: [`f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig`](https://github.com/f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig)
29	- `Emacs`: [`ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex`](https://github.com/ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex#inverse-search)
30	
31	---
32	
33	## SumatraPDF
34	
35	We highly recommend [SumatraPDF](https://www.sumatrapdfreader.org) on Windows
36	because Adobe Reader locks the opened PDF file and will therefore prevent further builds.
37	
38	### Forward Search
39	
40	Add the following lines to your editor config:
41	
42	```json
43	{
44	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "C:/Users/{User}/AppData/Local/SumatraPDF/SumatraPDF.exe",
45	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": [
46	    "-reuse-instance",
47	    "%p",
48	    "-forward-search",
49	    "%f",
50	    "%l"
51	  ]
52	}
53	```
54	
55	### Inverse Search
56	
57	Add the following line to your SumatraPDF settings file (Menu -> Settings -> Advanced Options):
58	
59	```ini
60	InverseSearchCmdLine = "texlab inverse-search --input "%f" --line %l"
61	```
62	
63	You can execute the search by pressing `Alt+DoubleClick` in the PDF document.
64	
65	---
66	
67	## Evince
68	
69	The SyncTeX feature of [Evince](https://wiki.gnome.org/Apps/Evince) requires communication via D-Bus.
70	In order to use it from the command line, install the [evince-synctex](https://github.com/latex-lsp/evince-synctex) script.
71	
72	### Forward Search
73	
74	Add the following lines to your editor config:
75	
76	```json
77	{
78	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "evince-synctex",
79	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["-f", "%l", "%p", "\"texlab -i %f -l %l\""]
80	}
81	```
82	
83	### Inverse Search
84	
85	The inverse search feature is already configured if you use `evince-synctex`.
86	You can execute the search by pressing `Ctrl+Click` in the PDF document.
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Okular
91	
92	### Forward Search
93	
94	Add the following lines to your editor config:
95	
96	```json
97	{
98	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "okular",
99	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--unique", "file:%p#src:%l%f"]
100	}
101	```
102	
103	### Inverse Search
104	
105	Change the editor of Okular (Settings -> Configure Okular... -> Editor) to "Custom Text Editor"
106	and set the editor command.
107	For `texlab inverse-search`, you can use the following command:
108	
109	```bash
110	texlab inverse-search -i "%f" -l %l
111	```
112	
113	You can execute the search by pressing `Shift+Click` in the PDF document.
114	
115	---
116	
117	## Zathura
118	
119	### Forward Search
120	
121	Add the following lines to your editor config:
122	
123	```json
124	{
125	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "zathura",
126	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--synctex-forward", "%l:1:%f", "%p"]
127	}
128	```
129	
130	### Inverse Search
131	
132	Add the following lines to your `~/.config/zathura/zathurarc` file:
133	
134	```bash
135	set synctex true
136	set synctex-editor-command "texlab inverse-search -i %{input} -l %{line}"
137	```
138	
139	You can execute the search by pressing `Ctrl+Click` in the PDF document.
140	
141	---
142	
143	## qpdfview
144	
145	### Forward Search
146	
147	Add the following lines to your editor config:
148	
149	```json
150	{
151	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "qpdfview",
152	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--unique", "%p#src:%f:%l:1"]
153	}
154	```
155	
156	### Inverse Search
157	
158	Change the source editor setting (Edit -> Settings... -> Behavior -> Source editor) to:
159	
160	```bash
161	texlab inverse-search -i "%1" -l %2
162	```
163	
164	and select a mouse button modifier (Edit -> Settings... -> Behavior -> Modifiers -> Mouse button modifiers -> Open in Source Editor)
165	of choice.
166	You can execute the search by pressing `Modifier+Click` in the PDF document.
167	
168	---
169	
170	## Skim
171	
172	We recommend [Skim](https://skim-app.sourceforge.io/) on macOS since it is the only native viewer that supports SyncTeX.
173	
174	Additionally, **disable** the "Reload automatically" setting in the Skim preferences (Skim -> Preferences -> Sync -> Check for file changes).
175	
176	### Forward Search
177	
178	Add the following lines to your editor config:
179	
180	```json
181	{
182	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "/Applications/Skim.app/Contents/SharedSupport/displayline",
183	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["-r", "%l", "%p", "%f"]
184	}
185	```
186	
187	If you want Skim to stay in the background after
188	executing the forward search, you can add the `-g` option
189	to `texlab.forwardSearch.args`.
190	
191	### Inverse Search
192	
193	Select your editor preset in the Skim preferences (Skim -> Preferences -> Sync -> PDF-TeX Sync support).
194	You can execute the search by pressing `Shift+⌘+Click` in the PDF document.
195	
196	---
197	
198	## Sioyek
199	
200	### Forward Search & Inverse Search
201	
202	Add the following lines to your editor config:
203	
204	```json
205	  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "sioyek",
206	  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": [
207	    "--reuse-window",
208	    "--execute-command",
209	    "toggle_synctex",
210	    "--inverse-search",
211	    "texlab inverse-search -i \"%%1\" -l %%2",
212	    "--forward-search-file",
213	    "%f",
214	    "--forward-search-line",
215	    "%l",
216	    "%p",  
217	],
218	```
219	
220	---

file.content:
`texlab` supports compiling LaTeX using a custom request (`textDocument/build`)
and by building a document after saving if configured to do so.
To enable building on save, simply set `texlab.build.onSave` to true.
Previewing can be configured in a variety of ways:

1. If you are using `latexmk`, you can create a `.latexmkrc` file and call your viewer accordingly.
   Afterwards, you can add the `-pv` flag to your `texlab.build.args`.

2. If you want the PDF viewer to stay synchronized with the cursor position in your editor,
   you can instruct `texlab` to execute a forward search after every build (`texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter`).
   To do so, you need to enable [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf)
   and update the `texlab.forwardSearch` configuration.
   If you want to use this feature, we do _not_ recommend the `-pvc` flag
   because `texlab` does not get notified by `latexmk` when a document gets built.
   Instead, you can use `texlab.build.onSave`.

In the following sections, we will give SyncTeX configurations for several popular viewers.
If your viewer is not listed here, you can send us a pull request or create an issue.


If you editor supports the `window/showDocument` request, 
then you can use `texlab inverse-search --input <FILE> --line <LINE>` to forward the inverse search to the editor.

In this section, we will give instructions for the `texlab inverse-search` subcommand. 
If your editor does not support it or the path of `texlab` is not fixed/known, then you need to adapt the commands to your editor.
Some plugins can help with setting up inverse search:

- `Neovim`: [`f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig`](https://github.com/f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig)
- `Emacs`: [`ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex`](https://github.com/ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex#inverse-search)

---

## SumatraPDF

We highly recommend [SumatraPDF](https://www.sumatrapdfreader.org) on Windows
because Adobe Reader locks the opened PDF file and will therefore prevent further builds.

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "C:/Users/{User}/AppData/Local/SumatraPDF/SumatraPDF.exe",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": [
    "-reuse-instance",
    "%p",
    "-forward-search",
    "%f",
    "%l"
  ]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Add the following line to your SumatraPDF settings file (Menu -> Settings -> Advanced Options):

```ini
InverseSearchCmdLine = "texlab inverse-search --input "%f" --line %l"
```

You can execute the search by pressing `Alt+DoubleClick` in the PDF document.

---

## Evince

The SyncTeX feature of [Evince](https://wiki.gnome.org/Apps/Evince) requires communication via D-Bus.
In order to use it from the command line, install the [evince-synctex](https://github.com/latex-lsp/evince-synctex) script.

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "evince-synctex",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["-f", "%l", "%p", "\"texlab -i %f -l %l\""]
}
```

### Inverse Search

The inverse search feature is already configured if you use `evince-synctex`.
You can execute the search by pressing `Ctrl+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## Okular

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "okular",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--unique", "file:%p#src:%l%f"]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Change the editor of Okular (Settings -> Configure Okular... -> Editor) to "Custom Text Editor"
and set the editor command.
For `texlab inverse-search`, you can use the following command:

```bash
texlab inverse-search -i "%f" -l %l
```

You can execute the search by pressing `Shift+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## Zathura

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "zathura",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--synctex-forward", "%l:1:%f", "%p"]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Add the following lines to your `~/.config/zathura/zathurarc` file:

```bash
set synctex true
set synctex-editor-command "texlab inverse-search -i %{input} -l %{line}"
```

You can execute the search by pressing `Ctrl+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## qpdfview

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "qpdfview",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--unique", "%p#src:%f:%l:1"]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Change the source editor setting (Edit -> Settings... -> Behavior -> Source editor) to:

```bash
texlab inverse-search -i "%1" -l %2
```

and select a mouse button modifier (Edit -> Settings... -> Behavior -> Modifiers -> Mouse button modifiers -> Open in Source Editor)
of choice.
You can execute the search by pressing `Modifier+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## Skim

We recommend [Skim](https://skim-app.sourceforge.io/) on macOS since it is the only native viewer that supports SyncTeX.

Additionally, **disable** the "Reload automatically" setting in the Skim preferences (Skim -> Preferences -> Sync -> Check for file changes).

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "/Applications/Skim.app/Contents/SharedSupport/displayline",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["-r", "%l", "%p", "%f"]
}
```

If you want Skim to stay in the background after
executing the forward search, you can add the `-g` option
to `texlab.forwardSearch.args`.

### Inverse Search

Select your editor preset in the Skim preferences (Skim -> Preferences -> Sync -> PDF-TeX Sync support).
You can execute the search by pressing `Shift+⌘+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## Sioyek

### Forward Search & Inverse Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "sioyek",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": [
    "--reuse-window",
    "--execute-command",
    "toggle_synctex",
    "--inverse-search",
    "texlab inverse-search -i \"%%1\" -l %%2",
    "--forward-search-file",
    "%f",
    "--forward-search-line",
    "%l",
    "%p",  
],
```

---

content.content:
1	This page describes the configuration settings that the server will query from the LSP client.
2	
3	---
4	
5	## texlab.build.executable
6	
7	Defines the executable of the LaTeX build tool.
8	
9	**Type:** `string`
10	
11	**Default value:** `latexmk`
12	
13	---
14	
15	## texlab.build.args
16	
17	Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured LaTeX build tool.
18	Note that flags and their arguments need to be separate
19	elements in this array.
20	To pass the arguments `-foo bar` to a build tool,
21	`latex.build.args` needs to be `["-foo", "bar"]`.
22	The placeholder `%f` will be replaced by the server.
23	
24	**Placeholders:**
25	
26	- `%f`: The path of the TeX file to compile.
27	
28	**Type:** `string[]`
29	
30	**Default value:** `["-pdf", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "-synctex=1", "%f"]`
31	
32	---
33	
34	## texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter
35	
36	Set this property to `true` if you want to execute a forward search after a build.
37	
38	**Type:** `boolean`
39	
40	**Default value:** `false`
41	
42	---
43	
44	## texlab.build.onSave
45	
46	Set this property to `true` if you want to compile the project after saving a file.
47	
48	**Type:** `boolean`
49	
50	**Default value:** `false`
51	
52	---
53	
54	## texlab.build.useFileList
55	
56	When set to `true`, the server will use the `.fls` files produced by the TeX engine as an additional input for the project detection.
57	
58	Note that enabling this property might have an impact on performance.
59	
60	**Type:** `boolean`
61	
62	**Default value:** `false`
63	
64	---
65	
66	## texlab.build.auxDirectory
67	
68	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the `.aux` files.
69	Note that you need to set the aux directory in `latex.build.args` too.
70	
71	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
72	
73	**Type:** `string`
74	
75	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
76	
77	---
78	
79	## texlab.build.logDirectory
80	
81	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the build log files.
82	Note that you need to change the output directory in your build arguments too.
83	
84	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
85	
86	**Type:** `string`
87	
88	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
89	
90	---
91	
92	## texlab.build.pdfDirectory
93	
94	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the output files.
95	Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too.
96	
97	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
98	
99	**Type:** `string`
100	
101	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
102	
103	---
104	
105	## texlab.build.filename
106	
107	Allows overriding the default file name of the build artifact. 
108	This setting is used to find the correct PDF file to open during forward search.
109	
110	**Type:** `string`
111	
112	**Default value:** `null` (by default, the server searches for `foo.pdf` if the main document is named `foo.tex`)
113	
114	---
115	
116	
117	## (DEPRECATED) texlab.auxDirectory
118	
119	Defines the directory containing the build artifacts.
120	Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too,
121	if you want to change the build directory.
122	In this case, use the `-outdir` flag for `latexmk`.
123	
124	**Type:** `string`
125	
126	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the TeX file)
127	
128	---
129	
130	## texlab.forwardSearch.executable
131	
132	Defines the executable of the PDF previewer.
133	The previewer needs to support [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf).
134	
135	**Type:** `string | null`
136	
137	**Default value:** `null`
138	
139	---
140	
141	## texlab.forwardSearch.args
142	
143	Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured previewer to perform the forward search.
144	The placeholders `%f, %p, %l` will be replaced by the server.
145	
146	**Placeholders:**
147	
148	- `%f`: The path of the current TeX file.
149	- `%p`: The path of the current PDF file.
150	- `%l`: The current line number.
151	
152	**Type:** `string[] | null`
153	
154	**Default value:** `null`
155	
156	---
157	
158	## texlab.chktex.onOpenAndSave
159	
160	Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after opening and saving a file.
161	
162	**Type:** `boolean`
163	
164	**Default value:** `false`
165	
166	---
167	
168	## texlab.chktex.onEdit
169	
170	Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
171	
172	**Type:** `boolean`
173	
174	**Default value:** `false`
175	
176	---
177	
178	## texlab.chktex.additionalArgs
179	
180	Additional command line arguments that are passed to [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
181	Don't redefine the `-I` and `-f` flags as they are set by the server.
182	
183	**Type:** `string[]`
184	
185	**Default value:** `[]`
186	
187	---
188	
189	## texlab.diagnosticsDelay
190	
191	Delay in milliseconds before reporting diagnostics.
192	
193	**Type:** `integer`
194	
195	**Default value:** `300`
196	
197	---
198	
199	## texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns
200	
201	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
202	If specified, only diagnostics that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
203	are sent to the client.
204	
205	See also [`texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsignoredpatterns).
206	
207	_Hint_:
208	If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
209	then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.
210	
211	**Type:** `string[]`
212	
213	**Default value:** `[]`
214	
215	---
216	
217	## texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns
218	
219	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
220	If specified, only diagnostics that match _none_ of the specified patterns
221	are sent to the client.
222	
223	See also [`texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsallowedpatterns).
224	
225	**Type:** `string[]`
226	
227	**Default value:** `[]`
228	
229	---
230	
231	## texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns
232	
233	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
234	If specified, only symbols that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
235	are sent to the client. 
236	Symbols are filtered recursively so nested symbols can still be sent to the client even though the parent node is removed from the results.
237	
238	See also [`texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsignoredpatterns).
239	
240	_Hint_:
241	If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
242	then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.
243	
244	**Type:** `string[]`
245	
246	**Default value:** `[]`
247	
248	---
249	
250	## texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns
251	
252	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
253	If specified, only symbols that match _none_ of the specified patterns
254	are sent to the client.
255	
256	See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).
257	
258	**Type:** `string[]`
259	
260	**Default value:** `[]`
261	
262	---
263	
264	## texlab.symbols.customEnvironments
265	
266	A list of objects that allows extending the list of environments that are part of the document symbols.
267	
268	See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).
269	
270	**Type:** `SymbolEnvironmentOptions[]`
271	
272	```ts
273	interface SymbolEnvironmentOptions {
274	    // The name of the environment.
275	    name: string;
276	
277	    // The name shown in the document symbols. By default, title case is used.
278	    displayName?: string;
279	
280	    // If set, the server will try to match a label to environment and append its number.
281	    label?: false;
282	}
283	```
284	
285	**Default value:** `[]`
286	
287	---
288	
289	## texlab.formatterLineLength
290	
291	Defines the maximum amount of characters per line (0 = disable) when formatting BibTeX files.
292	
293	**Type:** `integer`
294	
295	**Default value:** `80`
296	
297	---
298	
299	## texlab.bibtexFormatter
300	
301	Defines the formatter to use for BibTeX formatting.
302	Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent` or `none`.
303	
304	**Type:** `string`
305	
306	**Default value:** `texlab`
307	
308	---
309	
310	## texlab.latexFormatter
311	
312	Defines the formatter to use for LaTeX formatting.
313	Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent`, `tex-fmt` or `none`.
314	Note that `texlab` is not implemented yet.
315	
316	**Type:** `string`
317	
318	**Default value:** `latexindent`
319	
320	---
321	
322	## texlab.latexindent.local
323	
324	Defines the path of a file containing the `latexindent` configuration.
325	This corresponds to the `--local=file.yaml` flag of `latexindent`.
326	By default the configuration inside the project root directory is used.
327	
328	**Type:** `string`
329	
330	**Default value:** `null`
331	
332	---
333	
334	## texlab.latexindent.modifyLineBreaks
335	
336	Modifies linebreaks before, during, and at the end of code blocks
337	when formatting with `latexindent`.
338	This corresponds to the `--modifylinebreaks` flag of `latexindent`.
339	
340	**Type:** `boolean`
341	
342	**Default value:** `false`
343	
344	---
345	
346	## texlab.latexindent.replacement
347	
348	Defines an additional replacement flag that is added when calling `latexindent`.
349	This can be one of the following:
350	- `"-r"` 
351	- `"-rv"` 
352	- `"-rr"`
353	- `null`
354	
355	By default no replacement flag is passed.
356	
357	**Type:** string
358	
359	**Default value:** `null`
360	
361	---
362	
363	## texlab.completion.matcher
364	
365	Modifies the algorithm used to filter the completion items returned to the client.
366	Possibles values are:
367	- `fuzzy`: Fuzzy string matching (case sensitive)
368	- `fuzzy-ignore-case`: Fuzzy string matching (case insensitive)
369	- `prefix`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case sensitive)
370	- `prefix-ignore-case`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case insensitive)
371	
372	**Type:** `string`
373	
374	**Default value:** `fuzzy-ignore-case`
375	
376	---
377	
378	## texlab.hover.symbols
379	
380	Modifies the way symbol-like commands (e. g. `\epsilon`) are displayed during a hover request.
381	Possible values are:
382	- `none`: No hover is shown
383	- `glyph`: If available, the command is shown using a unicode character
384	- `image`: If available, a markdown image preview is returned. If not, the `glyph` method is tried next.
385	
386	**Type:** `string`
387	
388	**Default value:** `image`
389	
390	---
391	
392	
393	## texlab.inlayHints.labelDefinitions
394	
395	When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\label`-like commands.
396	
397	**Type:** `boolean`
398	
399	**Default value:** `true`
400	
401	---
402	
403	## texlab.inlayHints.labelReferences
404	
405	When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\ref`-like commands.
406	
407	**Type:** `boolean`
408	
409	**Default value:** `true`
410	
411	---
412	
413	## texlab.inlayHints.maxLength
414	
415	When set, the server will truncate the text of the inlay hints to the specified length.
416	
417	**Type:** `int | null`
418	
419	**Default value:** `null`
420	
421	---
422	
423	## texlab.experimental.followPackageLinks
424	
425	If set to `true`, dependencies of custom packages are resolved and included in the dependency graph.
426	
427	**Type:** `boolean`
428	
429	**Default value:** `false`
430	
431	---
432	
433	## texlab.experimental.mathEnvironments
434	
435	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as math environments (for example `align*` or `equation`).
436	
437	**Type:** `string[]`
438	
439	**Default value:** `[]`
440	
441	---
442	
443	## texlab.experimental.enumEnvironments
444	
445	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as enumeration environments (for example `enumerate` or `itemize`).
446	
447	**Type:** `string[]`
448	
449	**Default value:** `[]`
450	
451	---
452	
453	## texlab.experimental.verbatimEnvironments
454	
455	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as verbatim environments (for example `minted` or `lstlisting`).
456	This can be used to suppress diagnostics from environments that do not contain LaTeX code.
457	
458	**Type:** `string[]`
459	
460	**Default value:** `[]`
461	
462	---
463	
464	## texlab.experimental.citationCommands
465	
466	Allows extending the list of commands which the server considers as citation commands (for example `\cite`). 
467	
468	 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).
469	
470	**Type:** `string[]`
471	
472	**Default value:** `[]`
473

This page describes the configuration settings that the server will query from the LSP client.

---

## texlab.build.executable

Defines the executable of the LaTeX build tool.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `latexmk`

---

## texlab.build.args

Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured LaTeX build tool.
Note that flags and their arguments need to be separate
elements in this array.
To pass the arguments `-foo bar` to a build tool,
`latex.build.args` needs to be `["-foo", "bar"]`.
The placeholder `%f` will be replaced by the server.

**Placeholders:**

- `%f`: The path of the TeX file to compile.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `["-pdf", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "-synctex=1", "%f"]`

---

## texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter

Set this property to `true` if you want to execute a forward search after a build.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.onSave

Set this property to `true` if you want to compile the project after saving a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.useFileList

When set to `true`, the server will use the `.fls` files produced by the TeX engine as an additional input for the project detection.

Note that enabling this property might have an impact on performance.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.auxDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the `.aux` files.
Note that you need to set the aux directory in `latex.build.args` too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.logDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the build log files.
Note that you need to change the output directory in your build arguments too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.pdfDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the output files.
Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.filename

Allows overriding the default file name of the build artifact. 
This setting is used to find the correct PDF file to open during forward search.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `null` (by default, the server searches for `foo.pdf` if the main document is named `foo.tex`)

---


## (DEPRECATED) texlab.auxDirectory

Defines the directory containing the build artifacts.
Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too,
if you want to change the build directory.
In this case, use the `-outdir` flag for `latexmk`.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the TeX file)

---

## texlab.forwardSearch.executable

Defines the executable of the PDF previewer.
The previewer needs to support [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf).

**Type:** `string | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.forwardSearch.args

Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured previewer to perform the forward search.
The placeholders `%f, %p, %l` will be replaced by the server.

**Placeholders:**

- `%f`: The path of the current TeX file.
- `%p`: The path of the current PDF file.
- `%l`: The current line number.

**Type:** `string[] | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.chktex.onOpenAndSave

Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after opening and saving a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.chktex.onEdit

Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.chktex.additionalArgs

Additional command line arguments that are passed to [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
Don't redefine the `-I` and `-f` flags as they are set by the server.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.diagnosticsDelay

Delay in milliseconds before reporting diagnostics.

**Type:** `integer`

**Default value:** `300`

---

## texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
If specified, only diagnostics that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsignoredpatterns).

_Hint_:
If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
If specified, only diagnostics that match _none_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
If specified, only symbols that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client. 
Symbols are filtered recursively so nested symbols can still be sent to the client even though the parent node is removed from the results.

See also [`texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsignoredpatterns).

_Hint_:
If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
If specified, only symbols that match _none_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.customEnvironments

A list of objects that allows extending the list of environments that are part of the document symbols.

See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `SymbolEnvironmentOptions[]`

```ts
interface SymbolEnvironmentOptions {
    // The name of the environment.
    name: string;

    // The name shown in the document symbols. By default, title case is used.
    displayName?: string;

    // If set, the server will try to match a label to environment and append its number.
    label?: false;
}
```

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.formatterLineLength

Defines the maximum amount of characters per line (0 = disable) when formatting BibTeX files.

**Type:** `integer`

**Default value:** `80`

---

## texlab.bibtexFormatter

Defines the formatter to use for BibTeX formatting.
Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent` or `none`.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `texlab`

---

## texlab.latexFormatter

Defines the formatter to use for LaTeX formatting.
Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent`, `tex-fmt` or `none`.
Note that `texlab` is not implemented yet.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `latexindent`

---

## texlab.latexindent.local

Defines the path of a file containing the `latexindent` configuration.
This corresponds to the `--local=file.yaml` flag of `latexindent`.
By default the configuration inside the project root directory is used.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.latexindent.modifyLineBreaks

Modifies linebreaks before, during, and at the end of code blocks
when formatting with `latexindent`.
This corresponds to the `--modifylinebreaks` flag of `latexindent`.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.latexindent.replacement

Defines an additional replacement flag that is added when calling `latexindent`.
This can be one of the following:
- `"-r"` 
- `"-rv"` 
- `"-rr"`
- `null`

By default no replacement flag is passed.

**Type:** string

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.completion.matcher

Modifies the algorithm used to filter the completion items returned to the client.
Possibles values are:
- `fuzzy`: Fuzzy string matching (case sensitive)
- `fuzzy-ignore-case`: Fuzzy string matching (case insensitive)
- `prefix`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case sensitive)
- `prefix-ignore-case`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case insensitive)

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `fuzzy-ignore-case`

---

## texlab.hover.symbols

Modifies the way symbol-like commands (e. g. `\epsilon`) are displayed during a hover request.
Possible values are:
- `none`: No hover is shown
- `glyph`: If available, the command is shown using a unicode character
- `image`: If available, a markdown image preview is returned. If not, the `glyph` method is tried next.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `image`

---


## texlab.inlayHints.labelDefinitions

When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\label`-like commands.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `true`

---

## texlab.inlayHints.labelReferences

When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\ref`-like commands.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `true`

---

## texlab.inlayHints.maxLength

When set, the server will truncate the text of the inlay hints to the specified length.

**Type:** `int | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.experimental.followPackageLinks

If set to `true`, dependencies of custom packages are resolved and included in the dependency graph.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.experimental.mathEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as math environments (for example `align*` or `equation`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.enumEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as enumeration environments (for example `enumerate` or `itemize`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.verbatimEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as verbatim environments (for example `minted` or `lstlisting`).
This can be used to suppress diagnostics from environments that do not contain LaTeX code.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.citationCommands

Allows extending the list of commands which the server considers as citation commands (for example `\cite`). 

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands

Allows extending the list of `\label`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceCommands

Allows extending the list of `\ref`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceRangeCommands

Allows extending the list of `\crefrange`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes

Allows associating a label definition command with a custom prefix.
Consider,

```tex
\newcommand{\thm}[1]{\label{thm:#1}}
\thm{foo}
```

Then setting `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes` to `[["thm", "thm:"]]` and adding "thm" to `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands` 
will make the server recognize the `thm:foo` label.

**Type:** `(string, string)[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferencePrefixes

Allows associating a label reference command with a custom prefix.
See `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes` for more details.

**Type:** `(string, string)[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.glossaryReferenceCommands

Allows extending the list of `\gls`-like commands.

_Hint_: Additional commands need to be written without a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type**: `string[]`

**Default value**: `[]`

----

1	# Custom Messages
2	
3	We extend the [Language Server Protocol](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification)
4	with custom messages to provide better LaTeX integration.
5	These messages are _optional_ and it is up to the client to support them.
6	
7	## Build Request
8	
9	The build request is sent from the client to the server to build a given LaTeX document.
10	A `textDocumentBuild` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.
11	
12	_Request_:
13	
14	- method: 'textDocument/build'
15	- params: `BuildTextDocumentParams` defined as follows:
16	
17	```typescript
18	interface BuildTextDocumentParams {
19	  /**
20	   * The text document to build.
21	   */
22	  textDocument: TextDocumentIdentifier;
23	
24	  /**
25	   * The position of the cursor for use in forward search (optional).
26	   */
27	  position?: Position;
28	}
29	```
30	
31	_Response_:
32	
33	- result: `BuildResult` defined as follows:
34	
35	```typescript
36	interface BuildResult {
37	  /**
38	   * The status of the build process.
39	   */
40	  status: BuildStatus;
41	}
42	
43	enum BuildStatus {
44	  /**
45	   * The build process terminated without any errors.
46	   */
47	  Success = 0,
48	
49	  /**
50	   * The build process terminated with errors.
51	   */
52	  Error = 1,
53	
54	  /**
55	   * The build process failed to start or crashed.
56	   */
57	  Failure = 2,
58	
59	  /**
60	   * The build process was cancelled.
61	   */
62	  Cancelled = 3,
63	}
64	```
65	
66	## Forward Search Request
67	
68	The forward search request is sent from the client to the server when the user requests a forward search via SyncTeX.
69	A `textDocumentForwardSearch` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.
70	
71	_Request_:
72	
73	- method: 'textDocument/forwardSearch'
74	- params: [`TextDocumentPositionParams`](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification#textdocumentpositionparams)
75	
76	_Response_:
77	
78	- result: `ForwardSearchResult` defined as follows:
79	
80	```typescript
81	interface ForwardSearchResult {
82	  /**
83	   * The status of the previewer process.
84	   */
85	  status: ForwardSearchStatus;
86	}
87	
88	enum ForwardSearchStatus {
89	  /**
90	   * The previewer process executed the command without any errors.
91	   */
92	  Success = 0,
93	
94	  /**
95	   * The previewer process executed the command with errors.
96	   */
97	  Error = 1,
98	
99	  /**
100	   * The previewer process failed to start or crashed.
101	   */
102	  Failure = 2,
103	
104	  /**
105	   * The previewer command is not configured.
106	   */
107	  Unconfigured = 3,
108	}
109	```
110	
111	# Enum Mapping
112	
113	The following table describes the mapping of LaTeX and BibTeX structures
114	to their `CompletionItemKind` and `SymbolKind`.
115	
116	| LaTeX / BibTeX structure  | CompletionItemKind   | SymbolKind           |
117	| ------------------------- | -------------------- | -------------------- |
118	| Command                   | `Function` (3)       | `Function` (12)      |
119	| Command Argument          | `Value` (12)         | `Number` (16)        |
120	| Command Definition        |                      | `Key` (20)           |
121	| Snippet                   | `Snippet` (15)       |                      |
122	| Environment               | `Enum` (13)          | `Enum` (10)          |
123	| Section                   | `Module` (9)         | `Module` (2)         |
124	| Float                     | `Method` (2)         | `Method` (6)         |
125	| Theorem                   | `Variable` (6)       | `Variable` (13)      |
126	| Equation                  | `Constant` (21)      | `Constant` (14)      |
127	| Enumeration Item          | `EnumMember` (20)    | `EnumMember` (22)    |
128	| Label                     | `Constructor` (4)    | `Constructor` (9)    |
129	| Folder                    | `Folder` (19)        | `Namespace` (3)      |
130	| File                      | `File` (17)          | `File` (1)           |
131	| PGF Library               | `Property` (10)      | `Property` (7)       |
132	| TikZ Library              | `Property` (10)      | `Property` (7)       |
133	| Color                     | `Color` (16)         |                      |
134	| Color Model               | `Color` (16)         |                      |
135	| Package                   | `Class` (7)          | `Class` (5)          |
136	| Class                     | `Class` (7)          | `Class` (5)          |
137	| BibTeX Entry (Misc)       | `Interface` (8)      | `Interface` (11)     |
138	| BibTeX Entry (Article)    | `Event` (23)         | `Event` (24)         |
139	| BibTeX Entry (Book)       | `Struct` (22)        | `Struct` (23)        |
140	| BibTeX Entry (Collection) | `TypeParameter` (25) | `TypeParameter` (26) |
141	| BibTeX Entry (Part)       | `Operator` (24)      | `Operator` (25)      |
142	| BibTeX Entry (Thesis)     | `Unit` (11)          | `Object` (19)        |
143	| BibTeX String             | `Text` (1)           | `String` (15)        |
144	| BibTeX Field              | `Field` (5)          | `Field` (8)          |

file.content:
# Custom Messages

We extend the [Language Server Protocol](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification)
with custom messages to provide better LaTeX integration.
These messages are _optional_ and it is up to the client to support them.

## Build Request

The build request is sent from the client to the server to build a given LaTeX document.
A `textDocumentBuild` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.

_Request_:

- method: 'textDocument/build'
- params: `BuildTextDocumentParams` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface BuildTextDocumentParams {
  /**
   * The text document to build.
   */
  textDocument: TextDocumentIdentifier;

  /**
   * The position of the cursor for use in forward search (optional).
   */
  position?: Position;
}
```

_Response_:

- result: `BuildResult` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface BuildResult {
  /**
   * The status of the build process.
   */
  status: BuildStatus;
}

enum BuildStatus {
  /**
   * The build process terminated without any errors.
   */
  Success = 0,

  /**
   * The build process terminated with errors.
   */
  Error = 1,

  /**
   * The build process failed to start or crashed.
   */
  Failure = 2,

  /**
   * The build process was cancelled.
   */
  Cancelled = 3,
}
```

## Forward Search Request

The forward search request is sent from the client to the server when the user requests a forward search via SyncTeX.
A `textDocumentForwardSearch` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.

_Request_:

- method: 'textDocument/forwardSearch'
- params: [`TextDocumentPositionParams`](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification#textdocumentpositionparams)

_Response_:

- result: `ForwardSearchResult` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface ForwardSearchResult {
  /**
   * The status of the previewer process.
   */
  status: ForwardSearchStatus;
}

enum ForwardSearchStatus {
  /**
   * The previewer process executed the command without any errors.
   */
  Success = 0,

  /**
   * The previewer process executed the command with errors.
   */
  Error = 1,

  /**
   * The previewer process failed to start or crashed.
   */
  Failure = 2,

  /**
   * The previewer command is not configured.
   */
  Unconfigured = 3,
}
```

# Enum Mapping

The following table describes the mapping of LaTeX and BibTeX structures
to their `CompletionItemKind` and `SymbolKind`.

| LaTeX / BibTeX structure  | CompletionItemKind   | SymbolKind           |

## 1. 本讲目标

[u2-l1](u2-l1-config-overview.md) 教会了我们「配置归客户端、用三要素读懂任意一项、`%f`/`%p`/`%l` 怎么替换」这套通用语言。本讲把这套语言**第一次落到一个完整的子系统**——`texlab.build.*`，也就是「texlab 到底怎么把一个 `.tex` 文件编译成 PDF」。

读完本讲，你应该能够：

1. 用 `texlab.build.executable` / `texlab.build.args` **切换构建工具**（latexmk / tectonic / 裸 pdflatex 等），并能正确写出 `args`（flag 与参数必须拆成数组独立元素）。
2. 说清 `onSave`、`forwardSearchAfter`、`useFileList` 三个布尔开关**各自控制什么**，以及它们的性能与体验权衡。
3. 配置 `auxDirectory` / `logDirectory` / `pdfDirectory` / `filename` 这些「构建产物定位」项，并理解一个关键区分：**用 latexmk 时 texlab 会自动推断产物目录，非 latexmk 工具则必须你手动两头对齐**（既要在 `args` 里让引擎把产物放对地方，又要在这些设置项里告诉 texlab 去哪找）。
4. 认出已弃用的 `texlab.auxDirectory`，知道该用哪几项替代它。

`texlab.build.*` 是 Configuration.md 里**最常用**的一组配置，也是后续 [u3 编译与预览](u3-l1-build-and-preview-workflow.md) 整条工作流的地基。

## 2. 前置知识

本讲承接两篇讲义：

- **[u2-l1 配置总览](u2-l1-config-overview.md)**：你必须已经掌握「三要素（Type / Default value / Placeholders）」读法、占位符 `%f`/`%p`/`%l` 的含义，以及「flag 与参数要拆成数组独立元素」这条拆分规则。本讲会直接复用，不再从零解释。

本讲还要用到几个通俗概念：

- **构建工具（build tool）**：真正执行编译的程序。最常见的是 `latexmk`（一个自动决定要跑几遍、自动调用 bibtex/biber 的「构建管家」），也可以是 `tectonic`（自带包管理的一体化引擎）或最底层的 `pdflatex` / `xelatex` / `lualatex`。texlab 本身**不是**构建工具，它只是按你给的 `executable` + `args` 去**调用**构建工具。
- **构建产物（build artifacts）**：编译一次会生成一堆文件，除了最终的 `.pdf`，还有辅助文件 `.aux`/`.fls`/`.fdb_latexmk`、日志 `.log`/`.blg` 等。本讲的几个 `*Directory` 项就是告诉 texlab「这些产物分别放在哪个目录」。

> 提示：本仓库是 texlab 的**官方 wiki**（纯文档）。本讲的「源码」主要是 [Configuration.md](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md)，并会交叉引用 [Project-Detection.md](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Project-Detection.md) 与 [Previewing.md](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Previewing.md)。

| 文件 | 作用 | 本讲用到的地方 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Configuration.md` | 列出 `texlab.build.*` 全部 9 个配置项（含 1 个已弃用项） | 几乎所有源码精读取自这里（build.executable / args / onSave / forwardSearchAfter / useFileList / auxDirectory / logDirectory / pdfDirectory / filename / 已弃用 auxDirectory） |
| `Project-Detection.md` | 根目录检测四步法 | 说明 `.latexmkrc` / `Tectonic.toml` 如何同时决定**根目录**与**产物目录**，这是理解「自动推断」的关键 |
| `Previewing.md` | 编译与预览总览 | 说明 `onSave` 与 `forwardSearchAfter` 在端到端工作流里的位置，以及「为何不用 latexmk `-pvc`」 |

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

本讲拆成三个最小模块：

- **4.1 `build.executable` / `build.args`**——调用哪个构建工具、传什么参数。
- **4.2 构建触发：`onSave` / `forwardSearchAfter`**——什么时候编译、编译完要不要顺手跳一次正向搜索。
- **4.3 输出目录与产物定位：`auxDirectory`/`logDirectory`/`pdfDirectory`/`filename`/`useFileList`**——产物放哪、texlab 去哪找，以及 latexmk 的自动推断与已弃用项。

### 4.1 `build.executable` / `build.args`

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

texlab 自己不会编译 LaTeX。编译是这样一个过程：

```
texlab 拿到 build.executable + build.args
        │  （把 args 里的 %f 替换成当前 TeX 文件路径）
        ▼
   以子进程方式调用： <executable> <args...>
        │
        ▼
   构建工具（latexmk / tectonic / pdflatex ...）真正读 .tex、生成 .pdf 与各种产物
```

所以 `build.executable` 回答「**谁**来编译」，`build.args` 回答「**怎么**编译」（传哪些命令行参数）。这两项配套使用：换一个 `executable`，通常 `args` 也得跟着换。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

给定默认配置，一次编译实际执行的命令大致是：

```text
executable = "latexmk"
args       = ["-pdf", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "-synctex=1", "%f"]

# 假设当前文件是 /work/paper/main.tex，texlab 把 %f 替换后实际执行：
latexmk -pdf -interaction=nonstopmode -synctex=1 /work/paper/main.tex
```

三个默认 flag 各自的作用（这些是 latexmk / TeX 的通用知识，不是 texlab 发明的）：

| flag | 作用 |
| --- | --- |
| `-pdf` | 让 latexmk 用 pdflatex 产出 PDF（而非 DVI/PS） |
| `-interaction=nonstopmode` | 出错时不要停下来等用户输入，适合由程序驱动 |
| `-synctex=1` | 让 TeX 引擎写出 `.synctex.gz`，供 SyncTeX 双向跳转使用 |

切换构建工具时，`executable` 与 `args` 要一起改。例如换成 `tectonic`（详见 [u3-l3](u3-l3-tectonic-engine.md)），`args` 就不再是 latexmk 的 flag，而是 tectonic 的 CLI。换回裸 `pdflatex`，则要写成 `["-synctex=1", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "%f"]` 之类。

> 关键提醒（复用 u2-l1 的拆分规则）：`args` 是字符串**数组**，每个元素是命令行的一段。texlab **不会**替你按空格切分，所以 `-outdir build` 必须写成两个元素 `["-outdir", "build"]`，而不能写成 `"-outdir build"`。

109	3. 把 `texlab.build.executable` 改成 `pdflatex`，并把 `texlab.build.args` 改成（示例配置）：
110	   ```jsonc
111	   // 示例配置：改用裸 pdflatex
112	   "texlab.build.executable": "pdflatex",
113	   "texlab.build.args": [
114	     "-synctex=1",
115	     "-interaction=nonstopmode",
116	     "%f"
117	   ]
118	   ```
119	4. 再次保存触发编译。
120	
121	**需要观察的现象**：
122	
123	- 第 2 步：texlab 调用的是 `latexmk`（注意 latexmk 会自动跑多遍、自动处理引用）。
124	- 第 4 步：texlab 调用的是 `pdflatex`（只跑一遍，交叉引用可能需要手动多编译几次才能正确）。
125	
126	**预期结果**：你直观感受到「换 `executable` 就要换 `args`」。如果第 4 步你忘了改 `args`、仍带着 latexmk 的 `-pdf` flag 传给 pdflatex，pdflatex 会因为不认识 `-pdf` 而报错——这印证了二者必须配套。

128	> 具体编译是否成功取决于本机是否装了对应引擎，**待本地验证**。若没有 LaTeX 发行版，可改为「源码阅读型实践」：对照 [Configuration.md:30](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md#L30) 的默认 `args`，逐个 flag 查阅 latexmk 手册，说明每个 flag 的作用。
129	
130	#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案
131	
132	**练习 1**：默认配置下，texlab 实际执行的编译命令是什么？为什么零配置就能编译？
133	
134	> **参考答案**：执行 `latexmk -pdf -interaction=nonstopmode -synctex=1 <当前文件>`。因为 `build.executable` 默认 `latexmk`、`build.args` 默认已含 `%f`（见 [Configuration.md:30](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md#L30)），无需任何配置即可工作。
135	
136	**练习 2**：要让 latexmk 把产物输出到 `build` 子目录，`build.args` 里该怎么加？为什么不能写 `"-outdir=build"` 一个元素？（注：写成 `-outdir=build` 单元素 latexmk 其实能识别，但请按 texlab 的「拆分」规则思考另一种等价写法。）
137	
138	> **参考答案**：等价写法是 `["-outdir", "build"]` 两个元素。依据 [Configuration.md:18-22](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md#L18-L22)，texlab 不替你按空格切分，flag 与参数必须是数组独立元素。（`-outdir=build` 这种「等号连写」是单个 token，latexmk 能解析；但涉及「flag + 空格 + 参数」的形态必须拆分。）
139	
140	---
141	
142	### 4.2 构建触发：`onSave` / `forwardSearchAfter`
143	
144	#### 4.2.1 概念说明
145	
146	`executable`/`args` 定义了「**怎么**编译」，但还有两个问题没回答：「**什么时候**编译」和「编译完要不要顺手跳一次 PDF」。这正是 `onSave` 与 `forwardSearchAfter` 两个布尔开关。
147	
148	注意这两项默认都是 `false`——也就是说，**默认情况下 texlab 不会因为你保存文件就自动编译**。你需要显式打开 `onSave` 才有「保存即编译」的体验。
149	
150	#### 4.2.2 核心流程
151	
152	编译有两条触发路径（详见 [Previewing.md:1-3](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Previewing.md#L1-L3)）：
153	
154	```text
155	路径 A（手动/自定义请求）：
156	  编辑器 → 发 textDocument/build 自定义请求 → texlab 调用 executable+args 编译
157	
158	路径 B（自动/onSave）：
159	  用户保存文件 → （若 onSave=true）texlab 自动调用 executable+args 编译
160	```
161	
162	当 `forwardSearchAfter=true` 时，路径 B 会再接一步：
163	
164	```text
165	保存 → 编译 → （若 forwardSearchAfter=true）自动执行一次正向搜索（跳到 PDF 对应行）
166	```
167	
168	这样就串起了「**保存→编译→跳转**」的闭环。注意第二步跳转依赖 `forwardSearch.*` 配置（阅读器 executable + args + `%p`/`%l`），那部分在 [u3-l2](u3-l2-synctex-viewers.md) 讲。
169	
170	> **为什么不直接用 latexmk 的 `-pvc`（持续监视）？** Previewing.md 明确给出了原因：texlab **不会被 latexmk 通知**文档已编译完成（[Previewing.md:13-15](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Previewing.md#L13-L15)）。用 `-pvc`，latexmk 自己在那边一遍遍编译，但 texlab 不知情，于是 `forwardSearchAfter` 这类「编译后动作」无法可靠触发。所以官方推荐用 `texlab.build.onSave` 让**编译本身**由 texlab 主导。
171	
172	一句话决策表：
173	
174	| 想要的体验 | 配置 |
175	| --- | --- |
176	| 手动按键才编译 | 什么都不开（`onSave=false`，由编辑器发 `textDocument/build`） |
177	| 保存即编译 | `build.onSave = true` |
178	| 保存即编译 + 自动跳 PDF | `build.onSave = true` **且** `build.forwardSearchAfter = true`（外加 `forwardSearch.*` 配好阅读器） |
179	
180	#### 4.2.3 源码精读
181	
182	- [Configuration.md:44-50](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md#L44-L50)：定义 `texlab.build.onSave`——设为 `true` 则「保存文件后编译项目」，`Type: boolean`，`Default value: false`。
183	  - 这是「保存即编译」的总开关，默认关。
184	
185	- [Configuration.md:34-40](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Configuration.md#L34-L40)：定义 `texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter`——设为 `true` 则「编译后执行一次正向搜索」，`Type: boolean`，`Default value: false`。
186	  - 它必须与 `forwardSearch.*`（阅读器配置）配合，单独打开没有意义。
187	
188	- [Previewing.md:1-3](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Previewing.md#L1-L3)：确认编译的两条触发路径——自定义请求 `textDocument/build`，以及「保存后编译」（设 `texlab.build.onSave` 为 true）。
189	- [Previewing.md:9-15](https://github.com/latex-lsp/texlab.wiki/blob/bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd/Previewing.md#L9-L15)：说明 `forwardSearchAfter` 需要 SyncTeX 与 `forwardSearch` 配置配合，并**明确不建议**用 latexmk `-pvc`，原因如上。
190	
191	#### 4.2.4 代码实践
192	
193	**实践目标**：亲手体会 `onSave` 默认是关的，打开后才有「保存即编译」。
194	
195	**操作步骤**：
196	
197	1. 用一个能编译的工程，**先不配置** `build.*`（即 `onSave` 为默认 `false`）。
198	2. 修改 `main.tex` 内容并保存。观察是否自动编译。
199	3. 打开开关（示例配置）：
200	   ```jsonc
201	   // 示例配置：开启保存即编译
202	   "texlab.build.onSave": true
203	   ```
204	4. 再次修改并保存。
205	
206	**需要观察的现象**：
207	
208	- 第 2 步：保存后**没有**自动编译（除非你的编辑器扩展自己发了 `textDocument/build`）。
209	- 第 4 步：保存后**立即**触发一次编译，过几秒能看到 PDF 更新（或编译错误诊断）。
